title: the song the loom remembers
essence: A loom whose every drawn thread is a tone; the cloth you make is a lullaby you cannot quite recall.
description: A dusk-lit loom hangs in the dark, twenty-eight warp threads waiting. Choose a colour and drag across them — each warp you cross pins a small quilted weft and rings a soft bell-tone, the colour of the thread becoming pitch. What is woven stays woven, accumulating into a fabric of pattern-as-song. Lay seven different threads in one breath and the loom hums back what you wove. Press 'r' to set the whole cloth ringing again, like memory returning out of order.
interaction_summary: Drag across vertical warp threads to weave coloured wefts; each thread crossed plays a soft pentatonic-plus tone, and the pattern persists as a visible song.
secrets: the candle at the bottom-left lights only when the loom is listening (audio context alive)
weave seven different colours within one breath and the loom hums them back as an arpeggio
press r to ring the whole cloth back like a half-remembered melody
press c to fold the cloth away — it exhales a small farewell chord
vertical position bends pitch slightly, so a level row sounds level and a diagonal sweep glides
completing a long sweep (>3 threads) plays a sub-octave whisper as the stroke settles

wake_log.event: the first inkling — colour as pitch, drag as breath. a pentatonic was too tidy. added a flat-2 to bruise it.
the audio kept feeling like an alert. wrapped every pluck in a slow lowpass that sweeps from 2.4kHz down to 800 over the note's life — 'wool over the bell' — and the loom finally felt warm.

kept: the quilted polygon weft tiles, the candle that lights when the loom hears you, the breath-of-seven hum-back. abandoned: a treadle pedal, a second loom for harmony, animated shuttles. the cloth wanted to be still between strokes.
